resources.string: Info
Fast answers to the usual questions.
Search the info
Nothing matched. Try another word.
First steps
Setting up
Inside the game
Content & updates
About the app
Worlds & cleanup
Get in touch
Do I need to register?
Not at all. No login, no email, no password to keep track of. Just open the app and dive in.
Do I need to stay online the whole time?
Only for browsing the catalog and downloading. As soon as a mod sits on your phone, you can play without a connection (as long as Minecraft itself runs offline).
Which Minecraft versions are supported?
Android Minecraft Bedrock — the newest release plus a handful before it. Each mod spells out the exact versions it fits, look right by the file.
How do I set up a mod?
Open the mod, choose a file from the list, tap download, then install. Minecraft will launch and pull it in by itself. Final step — flip the pack on in the world you want to play.
And texture packs and skins?
Exactly like mods. Once installed, enable them in Minecraft — textures sit under Global Resources, skins under your Profile.
Why won\'t the mod install?

A few things to check first:
• The mod has to match your Minecraft version — see the version line on the mod page.
• Your phone needs free storage.
• Quit Minecraft and open it again (this fixes it more often than you'd think).
Still stuck? Re-download the file and try once more.
The download is frozen at 0% and won\'t move

If the progress bar refuses to move, your internet provider is most likely blocking the server the mods sit on — sometimes entire regions or carriers get cut off and there\'s nothing we can do from our end. Switch on a VPN, kick off the download again, and it should race through. Once the file is on your phone you can turn the VPN off — the mod itself doesn\'t need it.
I hit a \"file corrupted\" error
That means the download stopped partway. Delete what you\'ve got, fetch it again on a steady connection (Wi-Fi if possible) and you should be set.
I installed the mod but nothing shows up in the game
The mod is already in Minecraft, you just need to switch it on. Open your world\'s settings and enable the pack. A few mods also need Experimental Features enabled — same settings menu.
How do I enable Experimental Features?

While creating or editing a world, scroll to Experiments and flip on the toggle your mod asks for (the mod usually names it — often \"Holiday Creator Features\" or \"Beta APIs\"). Reload the world afterwards.
The game stutters after installing a mod

Some mods are simply demanding. Worth trying, in order:
• Close other apps sitting in the background.
• Lower the render distance in Minecraft (this helps the most).
• Nudge graphics quality down a step.
If it's still choppy, the mod may be too heavy for the device — go for a lighter one.
Can I run mods on a server with friends?
It depends on the server. Normally every player needs the same packs installed — if the server you\'re joining allows the mod, you\'re good. If it doesn\'t, the mod simply won\'t do anything there.
Do you keep adding new mods?
Constantly. Pull to refresh the catalog and you\'ll spot whatever landed since your last visit.
How do I tell if a mod was updated?
Whenever the mod author releases a new version, we bring it into the catalog. The version sits right beside the file on the mod page.
Are the mods safe?
They are. Every mod is checked before it lands in the catalog — no viruses, nothing shady tucked inside the file.
Why are there ads?
Servers and updates cost money, and ads are how we cover them — that\'s what keeps the app free for you. We do our best to keep them out of the way while you\'re actually using it.
It says \"no internet\" but mine is working
If your connection is fine and the app still complains, our servers are probably taking a short nap. Wait a few minutes and try again.
If I remove this app, do I lose my mods?
You don\'t. The mods now live inside Minecraft — they stick around even if our app is gone.
Will mods ruin my worlds?

Not by themselves. Mods are added as packs in Minecraft and you decide which world they go into. If you\'re testing a new mod and don\'t want to risk a world you care about — spin up a fresh world for it.
How do I remove a mod I\'m done with?
Inside Minecraft, open Packs (Behavior, Resource, or Skin depending on the type), long-press the one you want gone, and choose Delete. Your worlds stay untouched — only the mod goes.
Where do I let you know something\'s broken?
Use the Feedback section on the Suggest screen. Describe what happened — the more detail you share, the quicker we can crack it.
I built my own mod — can I send it over?
Absolutely. Open the Suggest screen and drop a link or attach the file there. We go through submissions and add the ones that fit.
